args.code:
import json
import pandas as pd

# Access the loaded data
books_var = locals()['var_functions.query_db:16']
reviews_var = locals()['var_functions.query_db:18']

print('Type of books_var:', type(books_var))
print('Type of reviews_var:', type(reviews_var))

# Check if they are file paths (strings) or actual data
if isinstance(books_var, str):
    print('Books var is a string (likely file path):', books_var[:100])
    # Try to load it as JSON file
    try:
        with open(books_var, 'r') as f:
            books = json.load(f)
        print('Successfully loaded books from file')
    except:
        print('Could not load books from file path')
        books = []
else:
    books = books_var
    print('Books var is not a string, using directly')

if isinstance(reviews_var, str):
    print('Reviews var is a string (likely file path):', reviews_var[:100])
    try:
        with open(reviews_var, 'r') as f:
            reviews = json.load(f)
        print('Successfully loaded reviews from file')
    except:
        print('Could not load reviews from file path')
        reviews = []
else:
    reviews = reviews_var
    print('Reviews var is not a string, using directly')

print('Books:', len(books) if isinstance(books, list) else 'Not a list')
print('Reviews:', len(reviews) if isinstance(reviews, list) else 'Not a list')

result = {'books_type': str(type(books_var)), 'reviews_type': str(type(reviews_var))}
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

While I think she could definitely have made a better impression on the family she worked for if she had been honest about her lack of French language skills from the beginning, she is a pioneer. Her drive and desire to learn the language from those in the actual country was inspiring. Not many people would have been gutsy enough to do what Linda did.

I also particularly enjoyed the relationship between Linda and Antoine. The Kind heart of the author was apparent in her actions toward the children, even when she struggled with them.

There is some romance, and I appreciated the way the author handled it. While sex scenes are not necessarily automatically offensive, there is something about memoirs that makes me uncomfortable if they are filled with them. This author manages to hint at her experiences without going into too much detail, leaving the reader to decide exactly what happened. Great writing.

When you step into reading this book, you learn a lot about French customs, French cuisine and wine. You also get the opportunity to brush up on your French skills a bit and get to step outside yourself and live an adventure through the eyes of the author. I read this book in one sitting with very few breaks in between and found myself enthralled in the sights, sounds and atmosphere of this memoir.

In the end, I was disappointed that the book ended. I could have kept reading about Linda's experiences without stopping anytime in the foreseeable future. The end of the book leaves many unanswered questions, that I am assuming the author will be answering in her next book. Were this a standalone, I would be very disappointed with the way the book ended, but with the knowledge that she is writing another book to continue with her story, I can't fault this one. One thing is for sure, if you read this book, you will be anxiously awaiting the next.

I would recommend this to anyone who likes books about travel, memoirs or just a good story that came from the recollections and heart of the author. Read it, you will be glad you did. Overall, this was a VERY enjoyable read that gave me many reasons to wish I could drop everything and head off to France!

args.query: SELECT purchase_id, AVG(CAST(rating AS FLOAT)) as avg_rating, COUNT(*) as review_count FROM review GROUP BY purchase_id HAVING AVG(CAST(rating AS FLOAT)) = 5.0 AND COUNT(*) >= 1

exec_args.sql: SELECT purchase_id, AVG(CAST(rating AS FLOAT)) as avg_rating, COUNT(*) as review_count FROM review GROUP BY purchase_id HAVING AVG(CAST(rating AS FLOAT)) = 5.0 AND COUNT(*) >= 1

var_functions.query_db:10.title: Ha! On me!  I thought this was a cookbook!
var_functions.query_db:10.text:
Lucky for me it's more than a cookbook.

The story of a modern Daniel Boone, growing up in Michigan, one of three sons of a hunter. Beginning at age eight when he shot a squirrel, to age 13 his first deer, then hunting his way through college, a career as a fur trapper, hunting sheep in Alaska, canoeing the Missouri River for deer and ending in Brooklyn age 37. Brooklyn?

I especially liked that I share his disgust at catch & release fishing - if you fish it, you have to eat it - anything else is sadism (my words).

My brother in law is the cook in the family and I wanted to see if this would be a good gift for him and I struck pay dirt following each chapter are "Tasting Notes" where the author speaks of cooking wild game.

I also enjoyed it for the history of the land he has hunted as well as the history of the hunt (I too was a big Daniel Boone fan growing up!).

I think more pictures (everything but dead things) would have made this a keeper.

var_functions.query_db:10.title: Four Stars
var_functions.query_db:10.text: Not as developed as Stephanie but I like the characters so far.

var_functions.query_db:10.title: A wonderful adventure in France
var_functions.query_db:10.text:
I loved this book all the way until the end. I have recently discovered that the author is intending to release another book, and from what I understand it will continue where this one left off. I am thankful for this, as the way this book ended was definitely a negative for me. I wanted to know more! What happened to the author, did she learn what she came to learn? Did she get the job she was hoping for? So many questions.

From the beginning of Linda's book, it is easy to love her. She is open, honest and definitely has the type of personality you want your heroine to have, whether the book is fiction or reality. I couldn't help but root for Linda throughout her trials with her host family, even when I thought she acted as a bit of a brat herself. Listening to her internal thoughts about what she went through and her desire to achieve her goals made this book feel close to my heart.

I applaud the adventurous spirit of the author and her decision to keep journal entries from that chapter of her life. What great material to have later to inspire a book! While I enjoyed the entire memoir, my favourite part of this book would have to be the author's descriptions of the many places she visited and the people she met along the way. While I think she could definitely have made a better impression on the family she worked for if she had been honest about her lack of French language skills from the beginning, she is a pioneer. Her drive and desire to learn the language from those in the actual country was inspiring. Not many people would have been gutsy enough to do what Linda did.

I also particularly enjoyed the relationship between Linda and Antoine. The Kind heart of the author was apparent in her actions toward the children, even when she struggled with them.

There is some romance, and I appreciated the way the author handled it. While sex scenes are not necessarily automatically offensive, there is something about memoirs that makes me uncomfortable if they are filled with them. This author manages to hint at her experiences without going into too much detail, leaving the reader to decide exactly what happened. Great writing.

When you step into reading this book, you learn a lot about French customs, French cuisine and wine. You also get the opportunity to brush up on your French skills a bit and get to step outside yourself and live an adventure through the eyes of the author. I read this book in one sitting with very few breaks in between and found myself enthralled in the sights, sounds and atmosphere of this memoir.

In the end, I was disappointed that the book ended. I could have kept reading about Linda's experiences without stopping anytime in the foreseeable future. The end of the book leaves many unanswered questions, that I am assuming the author will be answering in her next book. Were this a standalone, I would be very disappointed with the way the book ended, but with the knowledge that she is writing another book to continue with her story, I can't fault this one. One thing is for sure, if you read this book, you will be anxiously awaiting the next.

I would recommend this to anyone who likes books about travel, memoirs or just a good story that came from the recollections and heart of the author. Read it, you will be glad you did. Overall, this was a VERY enjoyable read that gave me many reasons to wish I could drop everything and head off to France!

args.code:
import json
import pandas as pd

# Get Literature & Fiction books
lit_books = var_functions.query_db:48

# Get perfect rating books
perfect_ratings = var_functions.query_db:50

# Since the first variable is likely a file path, let's read it directly
if isinstance(lit_books, str):
    with open(lit_books, 'r') as f:
        lit_books_data = json.load(f)
else:
    lit_books_data = lit_books

# Second variable might also be a file path
if isinstance(perfect_ratings, str):
    with open(perfect_ratings, 'r') as f:
        perfect_ratings_data = json.load(f)
else:
    perfect_ratings_data = perfect_ratings

print('Literature & Fiction books:', len(lit_books_data))
print('Perfect rating books:', len(perfect_ratings_data))

# Create DataFrames
df_lit = pd.DataFrame(lit_books_data)
df_perfect = pd.DataFrame(perfect_ratings_data)

# Map book_id to purchase_id
df_lit['purchase_id'] = df_lit['book_id'].str.replace('bookid_', 'purchaseid_')

# Find intersection - books that are both Literature & Fiction AND have perfect rating
perfect_lit_books = df_lit[df_lit['purchase_id'].isin(df_perfect['purchase_id'])]

print('Perfect rating Literature & Fiction books:', len(perfect_lit_books))

# Prepare results
result_list = []
for _, book in perfect_lit_books.iterrows():
    # Get review info from perfect_ratings
    review_info = df_perfect[df_perfect['purchase_id'] == book['purchase_id']].iloc[0]
    result_list.append({
        'title': book['title'],
        'book_id': book['book_id'],
        'categories': book['categories'],
        'average_rating': float(review_info['avg_rating']),
        'total_reviews': int(review_info['review_count'])
    })

result = {
    'perfect_rating_books': result_list
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result, indent=2))

var_functions.query_db:10.title: Ha! On me!  I thought this was a cookbook!
var_functions.query_db:10.text:
Lucky for me it's more than a cookbook.

The story of a modern Daniel Boone, growing up in Michigan, one of three sons of a hunter. Beginning at age eight when he shot a squirrel, to age 13 his first deer, then hunting his way through college, a career as a fur trapper, hunting sheep in Alaska, canoeing the Missouri River for deer and ending in Brooklyn age 37. Brooklyn?

I especially liked that I share his disgust at catch & release fishing - if you fish it, you have to eat it - anything else is sadism (my words).

My brother in law is the cook in the family and I wanted to see if this would be a good gift for him and I struck pay dirt following each chapter are "Tasting Notes" where the author speaks of cooking wild game.

I also enjoyed it for the history of the land he has hunted as well as the history of the hunt (I too was a big Daniel Boone fan growing up!).

I think more pictures (everything but dead things) would have made this a keeper.

var_functions.query_db:10.title: Four Stars
var_functions.query_db:10.text: Not as developed as Stephanie but I like the characters so far.

var_functions.query_db:10.title: A wonderful adventure in France
var_functions.query_db:10.text:
I loved this book all the way until the end. I have recently discovered that the author is intending to release another book, and from what I understand it will continue where this one left off. I am thankful for this, as the way this book ended was definitely a negative for me. I wanted to know more! What happened to the author, did she learn what she came to learn? Did she get the job she was hoping for? So many questions.

From the beginning of Linda's book, it is easy to love her. She is open, honest and definitely has the type of personality you want your heroine to have, whether the book is fiction or reality. I couldn't help but root for Linda throughout her trials with her host family, even when I thought she acted as a bit of a brat herself. Listening to her internal thoughts about what she went through and her desire to achieve her goals made this book feel close to my heart.

I applaud the adventurous spirit of the author and her decision to keep journal entries from that chapter of her life. What great material to have later to inspire a book! While I enjoyed the entire memoir, my favourite part of this book would have to be the author's descriptions of the many places she visited and the people she met along the way. While I think she could definitely have made a better impression on the family she worked for if she had been honest about her lack of French language skills from the beginning, she is a pioneer. Her drive and desire to learn the language from those in the actual country was inspiring. Not many people would have been gutsy enough to do what Linda did.

I also particularly enjoyed the relationship between Linda and Antoine. The Kind heart of the author was apparent in her actions toward the children, even when she struggled with them.

There is some romance, and I appreciated the way the author handled it. While sex scenes are not necessarily automatically offensive, there is something about memoirs that makes me uncomfortable if they are filled with them. This author manages to hint at her experiences without going into too much detail, leaving the reader to decide exactly what happened. Great writing.

When you step into reading this book, you learn a lot about French customs, French cuisine and wine. You also get the opportunity to brush up on your French skills a bit and get to step outside yourself and live an adventure through the eyes of the author. I read this book in one sitting with very few breaks in between and found myself enthralled in the sights, sounds and atmosphere of this memoir.

In the end, I was disappointed that the book ended. I could have kept reading about Linda's experiences without stopping anytime in the foreseeable future. The end of the book leaves many unanswered questions, that I am assuming the author will be answering in her next book. Were this a standalone, I would be very disappointed with the way the book ended, but with the knowledge that she is writing another book to continue with her story, I can't fault this one. One thing is for sure, if you read this book, you will be anxiously awaiting the next.

I would recommend this to anyone who likes books about travel, memoirs or just a good story that came from the recollections and heart of the author. Read it, you will be glad you did. Overall, this was a VERY enjoyable read that gave me many reasons to wish I could drop everything and head off to France!

import json
import pandas as pd

# First query result - English Literature & Fiction books
lit_books_path = var_functions.query_db:48

# Second query result - Books with perfect 5.0 ratings
perfect_ratings_path = var_functions.query_db:50

# Load the data from file paths
print('Loading Literature & Fiction books...')
with open(lit_books_path, 'r') as f:
    lit_books_data = json.load(f)

print('Loading perfect rating books...')  
with open(perfect_ratings_path, 'r') as f:
    perfect_ratings_data = json.load(f)

# Create DataFrames
df_lit = pd.DataFrame(lit_books_data)
df_perfect = pd.DataFrame(perfect_ratings_data)

# Map book_id to purchase_id in literature books
df_lit['purchase_id'] = df_lit['book_id'].str.replace('bookid_', 'purchaseid_')

print(f'Total English Literature & Fiction books: {len(df_lit)}')
print(f'Total books with perfect 5.0 rating: {len(df_perfect)}')

# Find books that are both Literature & Fiction AND have perfect rating
perfect_lit_books = df_lit[df_lit['purchase_id'].isin(df_perfect['purchase_id'])]

print(f'Perfect rating Literature & Fiction books: {len(perfect_lit_books)}')

# Prepare final results
result_list = []
for _, book in perfect_lit_books.iterrows():
    # Get review statistics
    review_info = df_perfect[df_perfect['purchase_id'] == book['purchase_id']].iloc[0]
    result_list.append({

var_functions.query_db:10.title: Ha! On me!  I thought this was a cookbook!
var_functions.query_db:10.text:
Lucky for me it's more than a cookbook.

The story of a modern Daniel Boone, growing up in Michigan, one of three sons of a hunter. Beginning at age eight when he shot a squirrel, to age 13 his first deer, then hunting his way through college, a career as a fur trapper, hunting sheep in Alaska, canoeing the Missouri River for deer and ending in Brooklyn age 37. Brooklyn?

I especially liked that I share his disgust at catch & release fishing - if you fish it, you have to eat it - anything else is sadism (my words).

My brother in law is the cook in the family and I wanted to see if this would be a good gift for him and I struck pay dirt following each chapter are "Tasting Notes" where the author speaks of cooking wild game.

I also enjoyed it for the history of the land he has hunted as well as the history of the hunt (I too was a big Daniel Boone fan growing up!).

I think more pictures (everything but dead things) would have made this a keeper.

var_functions.query_db:10.title: Four Stars
var_functions.query_db:10.text: Not as developed as Stephanie but I like the characters so far.

var_functions.query_db:10.title: A wonderful adventure in France
var_functions.query_db:10.text:
I loved this book all the way until the end. I have recently discovered that the author is intending to release another book, and from what I understand it will continue where this one left off. I am thankful for this, as the way this book ended was definitely a negative for me. I wanted to know more! What happened to the author, did she learn what she came to learn? Did she get the job she was hoping for? So many questions.

From the beginning of Linda's book, it is easy to love her. She is open, honest and definitely has the type of personality you want your heroine to have, whether the book is fiction or reality. I couldn't help but root for Linda throughout her trials with her host family, even when I thought she acted as a bit of a brat herself. Listening to her internal thoughts about what she went through and her desire to achieve her goals made this book feel close to my heart.

I applaud the adventurous spirit of the author and her decision to keep journal entries from that chapter of her life. What great material to have later to inspire a book! While I enjoyed the entire memoir, my favourite part of this book would have to be the author's descriptions of the many places she visited and the people she met along the way. While I think she could definitely have made a better impression on the family she worked for if she had been honest about her lack of French language skills from the beginning, she is a pioneer. Her drive and desire to learn the language from those in the actual country was inspiring. Not many people would have been gutsy enough to do what Linda did.

I also particularly enjoyed the relationship between Linda and Antoine. The Kind heart of the author was apparent in her actions toward the children, even when she struggled with them.

There is some romance, and I appreciated the way the author handled it. While sex scenes are not necessarily automatically offensive, there is something about memoirs that makes me uncomfortable if they are filled with them. This author manages to hint at her experiences without going into too much detail, leaving the reader to decide exactly what happened. Great writing.

When you step into reading this book, you learn a lot about French customs, French cuisine and wine. You also get the opportunity to brush up on your French skills a bit and get to step outside yourself and live an adventure through the eyes of the author. I read this book in one sitting with very few breaks in between and found myself enthralled in the sights, sounds and atmosphere of this memoir.

In the end, I was disappointed that the book ended. I could have kept reading about Linda's experiences without stopping anytime in the foreseeable future. The end of the book leaves many unanswered questions, that I am assuming the author will be answering in her next book. Were this a standalone, I would be very disappointed with the way the book ended, but with the knowledge that she is writing another book to continue with her story, I can't fault this one. One thing is for sure, if you read this book, you will be anxiously awaiting the next.

I would recommend this to anyone who likes books about travel, memoirs or just a good story that came from the recollections and heart of the author. Read it, you will be glad you did. Overall, this was a VERY enjoyable read that gave me many reasons to wish I could drop everything and head off to France!

val_args.code:
import json
import pandas as pd

# First query result - English Literature & Fiction books
lit_books_path = var_functions.query_db:48

# Second query result - Books with perfect 5.0 ratings
perfect_ratings_path = var_functions.query_db:50

# Load the data from file paths
print('Loading Literature & Fiction books...')
with open(lit_books_path, 'r') as f:
    lit_books_data = json.load(f)

print('Loading perfect rating books...')  
with open(perfect_ratings_path, 'r') as f:
    perfect_ratings_data = json.load(f)

# Create DataFrames
df_lit = pd.DataFrame(lit_books_data)
df_perfect = pd.DataFrame(perfect_ratings_data)

# Map book_id to purchase_id in literature books
df_lit['purchase_id'] = df_lit['book_id'].str.replace('bookid_', 'purchaseid_')

print(f'Total English Literature & Fiction books: {len(df_lit)}')
print(f'Total books with perfect 5.0 rating: {len(df_perfect)}')

# Find books that are both Literature & Fiction AND have perfect rating
perfect_lit_books = df_lit[df_lit['purchase_id'].isin(df_perfect['purchase_id'])]

print(f'Perfect rating Literature & Fiction books: {len(perfect_lit_books)}')

# Prepare final results
result_list = []
for _, book in perfect_lit_books.iterrows():
    # Get review statistics
    review_info = df_perfect[df_perfect['purchase_id'] == book['purchase_id']].iloc[0]
    result_list.append({
        'title': book['title'],
        'book_id': book['book_id'],
        'categories': book['categories'],
        'average_rating': float(review_info['avg_rating']),
        'total_reviews': int(review_info['review_count'])
    })

# Sort by review count (descending) for better presentation
result_list.sort(key=lambda x: x['total_reviews'], reverse=True)

result = {
    'perfect_rating_books': result_list,
    'count': len(result_list)
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result, indent=2))

args.code:
import json
import pandas as pd

# The query results are stored as file paths in these variables
books_file = 'var_functions.query_db:48'
ratings_file = 'var_functions.query_db:50'

# Load data from file paths
books_path = str(books_file)
ratings_path = str(ratings_file)

print('Loading from paths:')
print('Books:', books_path)
print('Ratings:', ratings_path)

# Read JSON files
with open(books_path, 'r') as f:
    lit_books = json.load(f)
    
with open(ratings_path, 'r') as f:
    perfect_ratings = json.load(f)

print('Loaded ' + str(len(lit_books)) + ' literature books')
print('Loaded ' + str(len(perfect_ratings)) + ' perfect rating books')

# Create DataFrames
df_lit = pd.DataFrame(lit_books)
df_perfect = pd.DataFrame(perfect_ratings)

# Map book_id to purchase_id
df_lit['purchase_id'] = df_lit['book_id'].str.replace('bookid_', 'purchaseid_')

# Find matches
matching_books = []
for _, book in df_lit.iterrows():
    purchase_id = book['purchase_id']
    perfect_match = df_perfect[df_perfect['purchase_id'] == purchase_id]
    if not perfect_match.empty:
        review_info = perfect_match.iloc[0]
        matching_books.append({
            'title': book['title'],
            'book_id': book['book_id'],
            'categories': book['categories'],
            'avg_rating': float(review_info['avg_rating']),
            'review_count': int(review_info['review_count'])
        })

result = {'matching_books': matching_books, 'count': len(matching_books)}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

val_args.code:
import json
import pandas as pd

# The query results are stored as file paths in these variables
books_file = 'var_functions.query_db:48'
ratings_file = 'var_functions.query_db:50'

# Load data from file paths
books_path = str(books_file)
ratings_path = str(ratings_file)

print('Loading from paths:')
print('Books:', books_path)
print('Ratings:', ratings_path)

# Read JSON files
with open(books_path, 'r') as f:
    lit_books = json.load(f)
    
with open(ratings_path, 'r') as f:
    perfect_ratings = json.load(f)

print('Loaded ' + str(len(lit_books)) + ' literature books')
print('Loaded ' + str(len(perfect_ratings)) + ' perfect rating books')

# Create DataFrames
df_lit = pd.DataFrame(lit_books)
df_perfect = pd.DataFrame(perfect_ratings)

# Map book_id to purchase_id
df_lit['purchase_id'] = df_lit['book_id'].str.replace('bookid_', 'purchaseid_')

# Find matches
matching_books = []
for _, book in df_lit.iterrows():
    purchase_id = book['purchase_id']
    perfect_match = df_perfect[df_perfect['purchase_id'] == purchase_id]
    if not perfect_match.empty:
        review_info = perfect_match.iloc[0]
        matching_books.append({
            'title': book['title'],
            'book_id': book['book_id'],
            'categories': book['categories'],
            'avg_rating': float(review_info['avg_rating']),
            'review_count': int(review_info['review_count'])
        })

result = {'matching_books': matching_books, 'count': len(matching_books)}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:10.title: Ha! On me!  I thought this was a cookbook!
var_functions.query_db:10.text:
Lucky for me it's more than a cookbook.

The story of a modern Daniel Boone, growing up in Michigan, one of three sons of a hunter. Beginning at age eight when he shot a squirrel, to age 13 his first deer, then hunting his way through college, a career as a fur trapper, hunting sheep in Alaska, canoeing the Missouri River for deer and ending in Brooklyn age 37. Brooklyn?

I especially liked that I share his disgust at catch & release fishing - if you fish it, you have to eat it - anything else is sadism (my words).

My brother in law is the cook in the family and I wanted to see if this would be a good gift for him and I struck pay dirt following each chapter are "Tasting Notes" where the author speaks of cooking wild game.

I also enjoyed it for the history of the land he has hunted as well as the history of the hunt (I too was a big Daniel Boone fan growing up!).

I think more pictures (everything but dead things) would have made this a keeper.

var_functions.query_db:10.title: Four Stars
var_functions.query_db:10.text: Not as developed as Stephanie but I like the characters so far.

var_functions.query_db:10.title: A wonderful adventure in France
var_functions.query_db:10.text:
I loved this book all the way until the end. I have recently discovered that the author is intending to release another book, and from what I understand it will continue where this one left off. I am thankful for this, as the way this book ended was definitely a negative for me. I wanted to know more! What happened to the author, did she learn what she came to learn? Did she get the job she was hoping for? So many questions.

From the beginning of Linda's book, it is easy to love her. She is open, honest and definitely has the type of personality you want your heroine to have, whether the book is fiction or reality. I couldn't help but root for Linda throughout her trials with her host family, even when I thought she acted as a bit of a brat herself. Listening to her internal thoughts about what she went through and her desire to achieve her goals made this book feel close to my heart.

I applaud the adventurous spirit of the author and her decision to keep journal entries from that chapter of her life. What great material to have later to inspire a book! While I enjoyed the entire memoir, my favourite part of this book would have to be the author's descriptions of the many places she visited and the people she met along the way. While I think she could definitely have made a better impression on the family she worked for if she had been honest about her lack of French language skills from the beginning, she is a pioneer. Her drive and desire to learn the language from those in the actual country was inspiring. Not many people would have been gutsy enough to do what Linda did.

I also particularly enjoyed the relationship between Linda and Antoine. The Kind heart of the author was apparent in her actions toward the children, even when she struggled with them.

There is some romance, and I appreciated the way the author handled it. While sex scenes are not necessarily automatically offensive, there is something about memoirs that makes me uncomfortable if they are filled with them. This author manages to hint at her experiences without going into too much detail, leaving the reader to decide exactly what happened. Great writing.

When you step into reading this book, you learn a lot about French customs, French cuisine and wine. You also get the opportunity to brush up on your French skills a bit and get to step outside yourself and live an adventure through the eyes of the author. I read this book in one sitting with very few breaks in between and found myself enthralled in the sights, sounds and atmosphere of this memoir.

In the end, I was disappointed that the book ended. I could have kept reading about Linda's experiences without stopping anytime in the foreseeable future. The end of the book leaves many unanswered questions, that I am assuming the author will be answering in her next book. Were this a standalone, I would be very disappointed with the way the book ended, but with the knowledge that she is writing another book to continue with her story, I can't fault this one. One thing is for sure, if you read this book, you will be anxiously awaiting the next.

I would recommend this to anyone who likes books about travel, memoirs or just a good story that came from the recollections and heart of the author. Read it, you will be glad you did. Overall, this was a VERY enjoyable read that gave me many reasons to wish I could drop everything and head off to France!

var_functions.query_db:10.title: Ha! On me!  I thought this was a cookbook!
var_functions.query_db:10.text:
Lucky for me it's more than a cookbook.

The story of a modern Daniel Boone, growing up in Michigan, one of three sons of a hunter. Beginning at age eight when he shot a squirrel, to age 13 his first deer, then hunting his way through college, a career as a fur trapper, hunting sheep in Alaska, canoeing the Missouri River for deer and ending in Brooklyn age 37. Brooklyn?

I especially liked that I share his disgust at catch & release fishing - if you fish it, you have to eat it - anything else is sadism (my words).

My brother in law is the cook in the family and I wanted to see if this would be a good gift for him and I struck pay dirt following each chapter are "Tasting Notes" where the author speaks of cooking wild game.

I also enjoyed it for the history of the land he has hunted as well as the history of the hunt (I too was a big Daniel Boone fan growing up!).

I think more pictures (everything but dead things) would have made this a keeper.

var_functions.query_db:10.title: Four Stars
var_functions.query_db:10.text: Not as developed as Stephanie but I like the characters so far.

var_functions.query_db:10.title: A wonderful adventure in France
var_functions.query_db:10.text:
I loved this book all the way until the end. I have recently discovered that the author is intending to release another book, and from what I understand it will continue where this one left off. I am thankful for this, as the way this book ended was definitely a negative for me. I wanted to know more! What happened to the author, did she learn what she came to learn? Did she get the job she was hoping for? So many questions.

From the beginning of Linda's book, it is easy to love her. She is open, honest and definitely has the type of personality you want your heroine to have, whether the book is fiction or reality. I couldn't help but root for Linda throughout her trials with her host family, even when I thought she acted as a bit of a brat herself. Listening to her internal thoughts about what she went through and her desire to achieve her goals made this book feel close to my heart.

I applaud the adventurous spirit of the author and her decision to keep journal entries from that chapter of her life. What great material to have later to inspire a book! While I enjoyed the entire memoir, my favourite part of this book would have to be the author's descriptions of the many places she visited and the people she met along the way. While I think she could definitely have made a better impression on the family she worked for if she had been honest about her lack of French language skills from the beginning, she is a pioneer. Her drive and desire to learn the language from those in the actual country was inspiring. Not many people would have been gutsy enough to do what Linda did.

I also particularly enjoyed the relationship between Linda and Antoine. The Kind heart of the author was apparent in her actions toward the children, even when she struggled with them.

There is some romance, and I appreciated the way the author handled it. While sex scenes are not necessarily automatically offensive, there is something about memoirs that makes me uncomfortable if they are filled with them. This author manages to hint at her experiences without going into too much detail, leaving the reader to decide exactly what happened. Great writing.

When you step into reading this book, you learn a lot about French customs, French cuisine and wine. You also get the opportunity to brush up on your French skills a bit and get to step outside yourself and live an adventure through the eyes of the author. I read this book in one sitting with very few breaks in between and found myself enthralled in the sights, sounds and atmosphere of this memoir.

In the end, I was disappointed that the book ended. I could have kept reading about Linda's experiences without stopping anytime in the foreseeable future. The end of the book leaves many unanswered questions, that I am assuming the author will be answering in her next book. Were this a standalone, I would be very disappointed with the way the book ended, but with the knowledge that she is writing another book to continue with her story, I can't fault this one. One thing is for sure, if you read this book, you will be anxiously awaiting the next.

I would recommend this to anyone who likes books about travel, memoirs or just a good story that came from the recollections and heart of the author. Read it, you will be glad you did. Overall, this was a VERY enjoyable read that gave me many reasons to wish I could drop everything and head off to France!

args.code:
import json, pandas as pd, os

lit_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:48']
perf_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:50']

print('Loading data files...')
print('Literature books file exists:', os.path.exists(lit_path))
print('Perfect ratings file exists:', os.path.exists(perf_path))

with open(lit_path) as f:
    lit_books = json.load(f)
with open(perf_path) as f:
    perfect = json.load(f)

print('Books loaded:', len(lit_books))
print('Perfect ratings loaded:', len(perfect))

# Create DataFrames
df_lit = pd.DataFrame(lit_books)
df_perf = pd.DataFrame(perfect)

# Map book_id to purchase_id
df_lit['purchase_id'] = df_lit['book_id'].str.replace('bookid_', 'purchaseid_')

# Find matching books
matches = []
perf_ids = set(df_perf['purchase_id'])
for _, row in df_lit.iterrows():
    if row['purchase_id'] in perf_ids:
        review_info = df_perf[df_perf['purchase_id'] == row['purchase_id']].iloc[0]
        matches.append({
            'title': row['title'],
            'book_id': row['book_id'],
            'avg_rating': float(review_info['avg_rating']),
            'review_count': int(review_info['review_count'])
        })

result = {'perfect_books': matches, 'count': len(matches)}
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

args.code:
import json

# The variables contain the actual data, not file paths
lit_books_data = locals()['var_functions.query_db:48']
perfect_ratings_data = locals()['var_functions.query_db:50']

print('Type of lit_books_data:', type(lit_books_data))
print('Type of perfect_ratings_data:', type(perfect_ratings_data))

# Process the data to find matching books
lit_books = lit_books_data if isinstance(lit_books_data, list) else []
perfect_ratings = perfect_ratings_data if isinstance(perfect_ratings_data, list) else []

print('English Literature & Fiction books:', len(lit_books))
print('Books with perfect 5.0 rating:', len(perfect_ratings))

# Create a lookup for perfect ratings
perfect_lookup = {item['purchase_id']: item for item in perfect_ratings}

# Find matches
matching_books = []
for book in lit_books:
    book_id = book['book_id']
    purchase_id = book_id.replace('bookid_', 'purchaseid_')
    if purchase_id in perfect_lookup:
        review_info = perfect_lookup[purchase_id]
        matching_books.append({
            'title': book['title'],
            'book_id': book_id,
            'categories': book['categories'],
            'average_rating': float(review_info['avg_rating']),
            'total_reviews': int(review_info['review_count'])
        })

result = {
    'match_count': len(matching_books),
    'books': matching_books
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:10.title: Ha! On me!  I thought this was a cookbook!
var_functions.query_db:10.text:
Lucky for me it's more than a cookbook.

The story of a modern Daniel Boone, growing up in Michigan, one of three sons of a hunter. Beginning at age eight when he shot a squirrel, to age 13 his first deer, then hunting his way through college, a career as a fur trapper, hunting sheep in Alaska, canoeing the Missouri River for deer and ending in Brooklyn age 37. Brooklyn?

I especially liked that I share his disgust at catch & release fishing - if you fish it, you have to eat it - anything else is sadism (my words).

My brother in law is the cook in the family and I wanted to see if this would be a good gift for him and I struck pay dirt following each chapter are "Tasting Notes" where the author speaks of cooking wild game.

I also enjoyed it for the history of the land he has hunted as well as the history of the hunt (I too was a big Daniel Boone fan growing up!).

I think more pictures (everything but dead things) would have made this a keeper.

var_functions.query_db:10.title: Four Stars
var_functions.query_db:10.text: Not as developed as Stephanie but I like the characters so far.

var_functions.query_db:10.title: A wonderful adventure in France
var_functions.query_db:10.text:
I loved this book all the way until the end. I have recently discovered that the author is intending to release another book, and from what I understand it will continue where this one left off. I am thankful for this, as the way this book ended was definitely a negative for me. I wanted to know more! What happened to the author, did she learn what she came to learn? Did she get the job she was hoping for? So many questions.

From the beginning of Linda's book, it is easy to love her. She is open, honest and definitely has the type of personality you want your heroine to have, whether the book is fiction or reality. I couldn't help but root for Linda throughout her trials with her host family, even when I thought she acted as a bit of a brat herself. Listening to her internal thoughts about what she went through and her desire to achieve her goals made this book feel close to my heart.

I applaud the adventurous spirit of the author and her decision to keep journal entries from that chapter of her life. What great material to have later to inspire a book! While I enjoyed the entire memoir, my favourite part of this book would have to be the author's descriptions of the many places she visited and the people she met along the way. While I think she could definitely have made a better impression on the family she worked for if she had been honest about her lack of French language skills from the beginning, she is a pioneer. Her drive and desire to learn the language from those in the actual country was inspiring. Not many people would have been gutsy enough to do what Linda did.

I also particularly enjoyed the relationship between Linda and Antoine. The Kind heart of the author was apparent in her actions toward the children, even when she struggled with them.

There is some romance, and I appreciated the way the author handled it. While sex scenes are not necessarily automatically offensive, there is something about memoirs that makes me uncomfortable if they are filled with them. This author manages to hint at her experiences without going into too much detail, leaving the reader to decide exactly what happened. Great writing.

When you step into reading this book, you learn a lot about French customs, French cuisine and wine. You also get the opportunity to brush up on your French skills a bit and get to step outside yourself and live an adventure through the eyes of the author. I read this book in one sitting with very few breaks in between and found myself enthralled in the sights, sounds and atmosphere of this memoir.

In the end, I was disappointed that the book ended. I could have kept reading about Linda's experiences without stopping anytime in the foreseeable future. The end of the book leaves many unanswered questions, that I am assuming the author will be answering in her next book. Were this a standalone, I would be very disappointed with the way the book ended, but with the knowledge that she is writing another book to continue with her story, I can't fault this one. One thing is for sure, if you read this book, you will be anxiously awaiting the next.

I would recommend this to anyone who likes books about travel, memoirs or just a good story that came from the recollections and heart of the author. Read it, you will be glad you did. Overall, this was a VERY enjoyable read that gave me many reasons to wish I could drop everything and head off to France!

args.code:
import json

# Get the stored results from previous queries
lit_books = locals()['var_functions.query_db:70']
perfect_ratings = locals()['var_functions.query_db:50']

# Parse the JSON data
lit_books_data = lit_books if isinstance(lit_books, list) else []
perfect_ratings_data = perfect_ratings if isinstance(perfect_ratings, list) else []

print('English Literature & Fiction books:', len(lit_books_data))
print('Books with perfect 5.0 ratings:', len(perfect_ratings_data))

# Create lookup for perfect ratings
perfect_lookup = {item['purchase_id']: item for item in perfect_ratings_data}

# Find matching books
matching_books = []
for book in lit_books_data:
    book_id = book['book_id']
    purchase_id = book_id.replace('bookid_', 'purchaseid_')
    if purchase_id in perfect_lookup:
        review_info = perfect_lookup[purchase_id]
        matching_books.append({
            'title': book['title'],
            'book_id': book_id,
            'categories': book['categories'],
            'average_rating': float(review_info['avg_rating']),
            'total_reviews': int(review_info['review_count'])
        })

# Sort by review count (descending) then title
matching_books.sort(key=lambda x: (-x['total_reviews'], x['title']))

result = {
    'total_matching_books': len(matching_books),
    'books': matching_books
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result, indent=2))

var_functions.query_db:10.title: Ha! On me!  I thought this was a cookbook!
var_functions.query_db:10.text:
Lucky for me it's more than a cookbook.

The story of a modern Daniel Boone, growing up in Michigan, one of three sons of a hunter. Beginning at age eight when he shot a squirrel, to age 13 his first deer, then hunting his way through college, a career as a fur trapper, hunting sheep in Alaska, canoeing the Missouri River for deer and ending in Brooklyn age 37. Brooklyn?

I especially liked that I share his disgust at catch & release fishing - if you fish it, you have to eat it - anything else is sadism (my words).

My brother in law is the cook in the family and I wanted to see if this would be a good gift for him and I struck pay dirt following each chapter are "Tasting Notes" where the author speaks of cooking wild game.

I also enjoyed it for the history of the land he has hunted as well as the history of the hunt (I too was a big Daniel Boone fan growing up!).

I think more pictures (everything but dead things) would have made this a keeper.

var_functions.query_db:10.title: Four Stars
var_functions.query_db:10.text: Not as developed as Stephanie but I like the characters so far.

var_functions.query_db:10.title: A wonderful adventure in France
var_functions.query_db:10.text:
I loved this book all the way until the end. I have recently discovered that the author is intending to release another book, and from what I understand it will continue where this one left off. I am thankful for this, as the way this book ended was definitely a negative for me. I wanted to know more! What happened to the author, did she learn what she came to learn? Did she get the job she was hoping for? So many questions.

From the beginning of Linda's book, it is easy to love her. She is open, honest and definitely has the type of personality you want your heroine to have, whether the book is fiction or reality. I couldn't help but root for Linda throughout her trials with her host family, even when I thought she acted as a bit of a brat herself. Listening to her internal thoughts about what she went through and her desire to achieve her goals made this book feel close to my heart.

I applaud the adventurous spirit of the author and her decision to keep journal entries from that chapter of her life. What great material to have later to inspire a book! While I enjoyed the entire memoir, my favourite part of this book would have to be the author's descriptions of the many places she visited and the people she met along the way. While I think she could definitely have made a better impression on the family she worked for if she had been honest about her lack of French language skills from the beginning, she is a pioneer. Her drive and desire to learn the language from those in the actual country was inspiring. Not many people would have been gutsy enough to do what Linda did.

I also particularly enjoyed the relationship between Linda and Antoine. The Kind heart of the author was apparent in her actions toward the children, even when she struggled with them.

There is some romance, and I appreciated the way the author handled it. While sex scenes are not necessarily automatically offensive, there is something about memoirs that makes me uncomfortable if they are filled with them. This author manages to hint at her experiences without going into too much detail, leaving the reader to decide exactly what happened. Great writing.

When you step into reading this book, you learn a lot about French customs, French cuisine and wine. You also get the opportunity to brush up on your French skills a bit and get to step outside yourself and live an adventure through the eyes of the author. I read this book in one sitting with very few breaks in between and found myself enthralled in the sights, sounds and atmosphere of this memoir.

In the end, I was disappointed that the book ended. I could have kept reading about Linda's experiences without stopping anytime in the foreseeable future. The end of the book leaves many unanswered questions, that I am assuming the author will be answering in her next book. Were this a standalone, I would be very disappointed with the way the book ended, but with the knowledge that she is writing another book to continue with her story, I can't fault this one. One thing is for sure, if you read this book, you will be anxiously awaiting the next.

I would recommend this to anyone who likes books about travel, memoirs or just a good story that came from the recollections and heart of the author. Read it, you will be glad you did. Overall, this was a VERY enjoyable read that gave me many reasons to wish I could drop everything and head off to France!

While I think she could definitely have made a better impression on the family she worked for if she had been honest about her lack of French language skills from the beginning, she is a pioneer. Her drive and desire to learn the language from those in the actual country was inspiring. Not many people would have been gutsy enough to do what Linda did.

I also particularly enjoyed the relationship between Linda and Antoine. The Kind heart of the author was apparent in her actions toward the children, even when she struggled with them.

There is some romance, and I appreciated the way the author handled it. While sex scenes are not necessarily automatically offensive, there is something about memoirs that makes me uncomfortable if they are filled with them. This author manages to hint at her experiences without going into too much detail, leaving the reader to decide exactly what happened. Great writing.

When you step into reading this book, you learn a lot about French customs, French cuisine and wine. You also get the opportunity to brush up on your French skills a bit and get to step outside yourself and live an adventure through the eyes of the author. I read this book in one sitting with very few breaks in between and found myself enthralled in the sights, sounds and atmosphere of this memoir.

In the end, I was disappointed that the book ended. I could have kept reading about Linda's experiences without stopping anytime in the foreseeable future. The end of the book leaves many unanswered questions, that I am assuming the author will be answering in her next book. Were this a standalone, I would be very disappointed with the way the book ended, but with the knowledge that she is writing another book to continue with her story, I can't fault this one. One thing is for sure, if you read this book, you will be anxiously awaiting the next.

I would recommend this to anyone who likes books about travel, memoirs or just a good story that came from the recollections and heart of the author. Read it, you will be glad you did. Overall, this was a VERY enjoyable read that gave me many reasons to wish I could drop everything and head off to France!
